citations.description: Injection oracle proves self-heal mechanism, but not repeated-run count
AC text: 'a single green run does not close a flake' — requires quantified run-count evidence not present in any repository file

findings.criterion: Demonstrate stability: the test passes across repeated xdist runs (quantified, e.g. 50/50 green) — a single green run does not close a flake.
findings.detail: This criterion demands a quantified runtime demonstration (e.g. 50/50 green runs under xdist) that cannot be verified from repository artifacts alone. The codebase contains the injection-based test (tests/interfaces/store/test_txn_transient_retry.py) which proves the self-heal mechanism works deterministically, and the fix is present in gitutil.py and event_append.py. However, the criterion explicitly states that a single green run is insufficient and calls for a quantified run-count result. No such run-count evidence is embedded in any repository file. The ticket's comment attestation ('579 passed; store+lifecycle tiers under -n 4 worksteal') is admissible only for operator-attested criteria, but this criterion is untagged (codebase-verifiable). The codebase bar is not met.
findings.remediation: Tag this criterion '[operator-attested]' and record a comment in the ticket that names the specific CI run IDs (or Gerrit Verified vote) where repeated xdist runs (e.g. 50 consecutive) all passed, along with the date observed. Alternatively, the existing Gerrit change 736 / commit d40b1a8 Verified +1 is a concrete attestation that, once the criterion is properly tagged, would satisfy it.

citations.description: Ticket comment: 'No regression: original failing test_transition_open_children.py 5 passed; store+lifecycle tiers under -n 4 worksteal 579 passed; make lint + make typecheck clean' — admissible as operator attestation only for a tagged [operator-attested] criterion

findings.criterion: `make test` (or the interface tier) green on all matrix cells.
findings.detail: This is a CI execution outcome (all matrix cells passing) that cannot be verified from repository artifacts. No CI run logs, test result files, or similar evidence is stored in the repository. The criterion is untagged (codebase-verifiable) but its evidence is inherently operational. The ticket's comment ('No regression: original failing test_transition_open_children.py 5 passed; store+lifecycle tiers under -n 4 worksteal 579 passed; make lint + make typecheck clean') and the Gerrit 'Verified +1' are concrete attestations, but are only admissible for operator-attested criteria.
findings.remediation: Tag this criterion '[operator-attested]' and record a comment with the specific CI run ID(s) or Gerrit Verified vote (change 736, commit d40b1a8 already has Verified +1) confirming all matrix cells passed, including date and which cells were green.

data.remediation: How to resolve the unmet criteria: for each one, decide whether the work is genuinely incomplete or simply undocumented. If a requirement is already satisfied but the proof is not discoverable from the code alone, add a comment to this ticket that documents the evidence — cite the concrete artifacts that meet the requirement (file paths and line ranges, commands and their output, links, or the reasoning that ties the work to the criterion). The completion verifier reads this ticket's comments, so evidence you record there is taken into account on the next verification. For any criterion whose work is genuinely unfinished, complete it, then re-verify.
